signals.idea: Add auto-scroll to the timeline when dragging clips or external items near any edge (top, bottom, left, right). Should work for internal clip drags (useClipDrag), external file/asset/text-tool drops (useExternalDrop dragover), and marquee selection (useMarqueeSelect). The scroll speed should accelerate as the pointer gets closer to the edge.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll coordination: wiring `createAutoScroller` only into pointer/drag handlers leaves marquee selection, snap/drop calculations, and drag indicators stale while RAF scrolling continues with a stationary pointer.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 2.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 1.

warnings: Scope creep detected: the plan appears to be expanding beyond the original idea or recorded user notes.

items: Dragging a clip near any edge of the timeline scroll container causes the container to scroll in that direction.
Scroll speed accelerates as the pointer moves closer to the edge (not constant speed).
External file/asset/text-tool drops trigger auto-scroll when dragged near edges during dragover.
Marquee selection extends correctly when the scroll container auto-scrolls (selection rectangle grows to encompass newly-visible clips).
Auto-scroll stops immediately when the drag/drop/selection ends (no lingering scroll).
Auto-scroll stops when the pointer moves away from the edge zone back into the center.
The drop-position indicator and snap calculations remain correct during auto-scrolling (no stale position bugs).
No visible jank or performance degradation during auto-scroll (single RAF loop, no duplicate timers).

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll coordination: wiring `createAutoScroller` only into pointer/drag handlers leaves marquee selection, snap/drop calculations, and drag indicators stale while RAF scrolling continues with a stationary pointer.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `createAutoScroller` only mutates `scrollLeft`/`scrollTop` inside its RAF tick and does not invoke any drag-state recomputation (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/auto-scroll.ts:14-23, :25-52). `useMarqueeSelect` recomputes the marquee only inside `handlePointerMove` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useMarqueeSelect.ts:93-145). `useClipDrag` only calls `coordinator.update(...)` from `handlePointerMove` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:251-279). `useDragCoordinator` has no scroll subscription; it updates position only when `update(...)` is called (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts:117-153). So Step 2's claim that calling `autoScroller.update(...)` before `updateSelection()` makes selection read the new scroll position is false against the current utility API.
